meaning: adj.退行的,逆行的,[遗]隐性的n.隐性性状
examples: Sickle-cell anaemia is passed on through a recessive gene.
This apparent anomaly is partially explained by the fact that most founder mutations are recessive: only a person with copies of the affected gene from both parents becomes ill.
The subspecies Acinonyx jubatus guttatus may also have been a variation due to a recessive gene.
The mild-eyed actor Ryan Reynolds is perhaps too recessive for starring roles in the movies.
Such stealth traits are readily handled by classic genetics--in the form of recessive genes.
